claim: NFAT4 activation requires IP3R-mediated Ca2+ mobilization.
evidence_sentences: Here, we show that two Ca2+-dependent transcription factor isoforms, NFAT1 and NFAT4, require distinct sub-cellular InsP3 and Ca2+ signals for physiologically sustained activation.
NFAT1 is stimulated by sub-plasmalemmal Ca2+ microdomains, whereas NFAT4 additionally requires Ca2+ mobilization from the inner nuclear envelope by nuclear InsP3 receptors.
Instead, effective sustained physiological activation of NFAT4 is tightly linked to oscillations in nuclear Ca2+.
Our results show how gene expression can be controlled by coincident yet geographically distinct Ca2+ signals, generated by a freely diffusible InsP3 message.
